title: nepeta caerulea subsp. sanabrensis
intro: Nepeta caerulea subsp. sanabrensis, also known as Spanish catmint or blue catmint, is a subspecies of the Nepeta caerulea plant, a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as nepetalactone, responsible for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Nepeta caerulea subsp. sanabrensis include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to ease respiratory problems and soothe insect bites. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
